mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly decline in unemployment rate below 4% signals tight labor market conditions that boost corporate profit margins through wage growth, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in 60%+ of instances where UNRATE remains below 4.0 for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A concurrent decline in both GDP growth (GDPC1) and housing starts (HOUST) signals weakening aggregate demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in 70%+ of periods where both GDPC1 and HOUST decline sequentially for two months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI reading above 3.5% triggers central bank tightening expectations, causing bond yield increases that reduce equity valuations and produce negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where CPIAUCSL exceeds 3.5 for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above 105 signals robust manufacturing activity that boosts corporate earnings, generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60d returns in 50%+ of periods where INDPRO remains above 105 for four consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly payroll gain (PAYEMS) exceeding 200k signals strong employment growth that increases consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where PAYEMS exceeds 200k for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous drop in both consumer credit (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates reduced private sector demand, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in 70%+ of periods where RSAFS and HOUST both decline sequentially for two months.

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) combined with declining GDP growth (GDPC1) signals a recessionary monetary policy, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in 60%+ of instances where FEDFUNDS rises and GDPC1 declines simultaneously.
